facts_disclosed: Switched to Bonsoy from Minor Figures

turns.content: i have been betrayed by a brand i trusted and i need to process this
That sounds serious. What happened — which brand and what did they do?

minor figures. the oat milk. i have been riding for them for ages like i was genuinely loyal, recommended them to everyone, would not shut up about how good their barista formula was for steaming. and then they changed it
Oh no, the dreaded formula change. That's always devastating when it's a product you've built your routine around. What's different about the new version — taste, texture, or both?

it splits in EVERYTHING now. like it used to steam perfectly and integrate beautifully with espresso and now it just curdles and separates and looks disgusting. the latte art is impossible because the foam is completely different — thinner and it doesnt hold. and its slightly sweeter in a way that messes with the coffee balance. i genuinely feel gaslit because the packaging looks the same

Same packaging with a completely different product inside is the worst kind of stealth reformulation. And the splitting is a total dealbreaker in a barista context — it's not just preference, it literally doesn't function anymore.

This happens frustratingly often with alt-milk brands. They reformulate to cut costs or change suppliers and wreck the properties that made baristas love them. Have you found a replacement, or are you still in the grief stage?

ok so i went through a brief chaos period where i tried like three different brands in one week and drove my manager slightly insane. but ive landed on bonsoy's oat milk and honestly?? its really good. steams well, doesnt split, the foam is creamy and holds shape. its slightly nuttier than the old minor figures was but in a way that actually works with our espresso blend

Oh nice, Bonsoy is a great shout! They've been solid in the soy game for years so it makes sense their oat would be well-formulated. And a slightly nuttier profile can actually complement espresso really well, especially with medium or darker roasts.

Can you do latte art with it? That's the real test.

YES thats what sold me. the microfoam is genuinely comparable to what old minor figures used to give me. i can do a rosetta with it which is my benchmark for whether an oat milk is acceptable lol. and it holds up even when the espresso is quite acidic which was always a splitting risk with other brands. ive been lobbying my manager to make it the house oat milk

The rosetta test as a quality benchmark is very practical — if the foam holds a rosetta, it's legit. And acid stability is huge because that's exactly where most oat milks fail.

How's the lobbying going? Is your manager on board or does she need more convincing?

shes warming up to it because customers have literally been commenting on drinks looking weird with the minor figures splitting. like someone sent a flat white back last week because it looked curdled and my manager had to comp it. so theres an actual business case now not just me being dramatic about milk. the cost is basically the same too so theres no real argument against switching

A comped drink is a pretty compelling business case — that's real money lost plus a bad customer experience. If the cost is comparable and the quality is visibly better, your manager would be making the practical choice. Sometimes these decisions need a catalyst and a curdled flat white being sent back is exactly that.

Do the other baristas have opinions on it, or is this mainly your crusade?

no cap everyone prefers the bonsoy now that theyve tried it. even the ones who dont care about latte art can tell the difference in how it steams — its just smoother and more predictable. one of the other baristas was like 'wait oat milk can actually do this??' because he'd only ever worked with the broken minor figures formula and thought oat was just inherently difficult lol

That's actually kind of sad — imagine thinking oat milk is just bad because your only reference point was a botched reformulation. At least now the whole team knows what good oat milk is supposed to behave like.

Sounds like the switch is basically a done deal at this point. Unanimous barista approval plus customer complaints about the old one is a pretty airtight case.

i just cant believe i was so loyal to minor figures for so long and they did this. if bonsoy ever changes their formula i am simply going to raise my own oats and make it from scratch. i refuse to go through this again lol ok thanks for letting me vent about milk drama bye

Homemade oat milk from your own oat farm — the only truly reliable supply chain. I respect the contingency plan.

Milk drama IS serious when you're a barista — it's a core work tool. Glad you found Bonsoy and that the whole cafe is getting behind the switch. Hope it becomes official soon. Take care!
